meta.title: Single Types - Global Configuration
meta.excerpt: Creating single-type collections for global settings, site configuration, and one-off content management.

seo.metaTitle: Single Types in Strapi | CMS Reference
seo.metaDescription: Master single-type collections for global content management.

blocks.content: Single types are perfect for managing global configuration options and site-wide settings that don't require multiple entries.
Single types have a complete schema definition similar to collections but with kind set to 'singleType'. Here's a comprehensive example for site settings.

blocks.title: Complete Site Settings Schema
Single Type Key Difference
blocks.content: Single types use 'singleType' as the kind value and typically have draftAndPublish set to false since there's only one instance.
Single types are ideal for site-wide configuration and one-off pages that don't need multiple entries. Here are common use cases.

features.title: Global Settings

blocks.title: Homepage Single Type
blocks.content: Manage hero content, featured articles, testimonials, and CTA sections for the homepage

blocks.title: Navigation Single Type
blocks.content: Define main navigation structure, footer menus, and mobile navigation configuration

blocks.title: Legal Content Single Type
blocks.content: Store privacy policy, terms of service, and GDPR compliance information

blocks.title: When to Use Single Types
blocks.content: Use single types for content that appears once across the site and doesn't need versioning or multiple entries.
Single types have a simplified API pattern compared to collections. There's no need for ID parameters since there's only one instance.

blocks.title: Fetching Single Type Data
Updating Single Type Data

items: No DELETE endpoint (single types always exist)
Use populate parameter to include relations and components
No pagination needed since there's only one entry

blocks.title: API Simplicity
blocks.content: Single type APIs are simpler than collection APIs since there's no need for ID management, pagination, or filtering.
